result:
**Goal: Add a Gravity N-Body simulation mode (Shift+K) that models gravitational interactions between massive bodies with real-time orbital mechanics, collisions, and trail rendering.**

This fills a notable gap — the project has electromagnetic particle physics (Shift+G) and flocking behavior (B), but no pure Newtonian gravity simulation. An N-body mode would let users spawn stars, planets, and moons that orbit each other via gravitational attraction, merge on collision, and leave orbital trails in the terminal. It pairs naturally with the existing particle-based modes and offers visually compelling emergent behavior (binary star systems, slingshots, chaotic 3-body dynamics) with relatively straightforward physics (F = Gm₁m₂/r²).

Want me to build it?
